title: Build & Manage a Campaign Dial List
lede: The dial list is your campaign's calling queue — the exact people your AI agent will call, in what order, and how many times. Learn how to load the right audience, read every column, control retries, and keep the list clean from first dial to finished campaign.

steps.title: Open your campaign
steps.content:
Every campaign has its own dial list, so the first stop is the campaign itself.

  1. In the top navigation, click Campaigns. The Smart Dialer sub-tab opens with your list of AI campaigns.
  2. Find your campaign in the table — the Contacts column already tells you how many people are queued, and Progress shows how far along the calling is.
  3. Click the campaign's name to open it.
💡 Don't have a campaign yet? Create one first — see Create a Campaign in the What's Next section below. You can build the dial list before or after configuring the agent and phone numbers.

images.alt: Campaigns list with the Smart Dialer tab active
images.caption: Campaigns → Smart Dialer: the Contacts column shows how many people each campaign will call

images.alt: Campaigns list with the Smart Dialer tab active
images.caption: Campaigns → Smart Dialer: the Contacts column shows how many people each campaign will call

steps.title: Know your way around the campaign tabs
steps.content:
Inside a campaign, everything dial-list related lives in four of the tabs across the top:

  • Dial List — the queue itself: who's waiting, who's been called, and each person's status.
  • Add Leads — pull leads from your CRM into the queue.
  • Add Contacts — same thing, but for CRM contacts.
  • Overview — live progress numbers for the whole list.

The other tabs — Settings (retry rules, working hours, pacing), Campaign Logs (every call placed), and Stages (custom lifecycle stages for this campaign) — shape how the list gets dialed.

images.alt: Campaign tab bar with Overview, Settings, Dial List, Add Leads, Add Contacts, Campaign Logs, and Stages
images.caption: The campaign tabs — Dial List, Add Leads, and Add Contacts are where the queue is built

images.alt: Campaign tab bar with Overview, Settings, Dial List, Add Leads, Add Contacts, Campaign Logs, and Stages
images.caption: The campaign tabs — Dial List, Add Leads, and Add Contacts are where the queue is built

steps.title: Add leads and contacts to the queue
steps.content:
Your dial list is built from the leads and contacts already in your CRM — no re-typing, no copy-paste. And everything on each record travels with it: name, phone, stage, and any custom variables, so your AI agent can personalize every single call automatically.

  1. Open the Add Leads tab (or Add Contacts — they work identically).
  2. The table shows every CRM record that is not yet in this campaign, with their Name, Phone, and Stage. Click View on any row to peek at the full record before committing.
  3. Tick the checkbox next to each person you want, then click Add Selected Leads (the button shows a live count of your selection).
  4. Adding a whole page? Tick the header checkbox — a prompt appears letting you Select all matching records across every page in one click.
  5. Click Refresh any time to re-pull the latest CRM data.
💡 Starting from a spreadsheet? Import your CSV into the CRM first (CRM → Leads or Contacts), then come back here and add them — see Import & Export Data below. Once imported, they show up in this tab instantly.

images.alt: Add Leads tab showing available CRM leads with checkboxes and the Add Selected Leads button
images.caption: The Add Leads tab — select people from your CRM and queue them with one click

images.alt: Add Leads tab showing available CRM leads with checkboxes and the Add Selected Leads button
images.caption: The Add Leads tab — select people from your CRM and queue them with one click

steps.title: Target the exact audience with filters
steps.content:
Calling the right people beats calling more people. The Filters button on the Add Leads / Add Contacts tab lets you slice your CRM before you add anyone:

  1. Click Filters in the top-left of the tab.
  2. Use the search box at the top — switch the field selector between Name, Phone, and Email.
  3. Tick any combination of filter types and pick values for each:
  • Lifecycle Stage — e.g. only "New" or only "Interested" records.
  • Source — where the lead came from (Website, Referral, Social Media…).
  • Account — contacts belonging to a specific company account.
  • City, State/Province, Country, Zip/Postal Code — geographic targeting.
  • Tags — any tags you've applied in the CRM.

Active filters appear as removable chips next to the button. Combine filters, then Select all — that's how you queue "every New lead in Texas tagged spring-promo" in three clicks.

images.alt: Filters dropdown with search field and filter options for stage, source, account, city, tags, state, country, and zip
images.caption: The Filters menu — stack stage, source, geography, and tag filters to zero in on your audience

images.alt: Filters dropdown with search field and filter options for stage, source, account, city, tags, state, country, and zip
images.caption: The Filters menu — stack stage, source, geography, and tag filters to zero in on your audience

steps.title: Read the Dial List — every column explained
steps.content:
Open the Dial List tab to see your queue. Each row is one phone number the campaign will call:

  • Entity Name — the lead or contact. Click the name to open their full CRM record in a side drawer without leaving the page.
  • Status — where this entry is in its journey: Pending (waiting to be dialed), Completed (all attempts used), or a stage-met completion shown in green.
  • Global Stage — the person's overall lifecycle stage in your CRM (New, Contacted, Interested…).
  • Attempts — dials made vs. allowed, e.g. 0/3 means three tries remain.
  • Target Phone — the exact number that will be dialed.
  • Call Stage — the stage recorded on this number's most recent call, so you can see the latest outcome at a glance.
  • Scheduled At — when the next attempt is due (shown in the campaign's timezone).

The header buttons matter too: Start launches the campaign, Export CSV / Export PDF download the campaign's call logs, and Refresh re-loads the table.

images.alt: Dial List tab showing queued entries with status, stage, attempts, phone, and schedule columns
images.caption: The Dial List: every queued person with their status, attempts used, and next scheduled call

images.alt: Dial List tab showing queued entries with status, stage, attempts, phone, and schedule columns
images.caption: The Dial List: every queued person with their status, attempts used, and next scheduled call

steps.title: Search and filter the queue
steps.content:
On a list with thousands of entries, the toolbar keeps you oriented:

  1. Use the quick-view buttons at the top: Show All, Show Pending (not yet fully dialed), or Show Completed (all attempts used or stage met).
  2. Type in Search by name or phone number… to jump straight to one person.
  3. Use Filter by lifecycle stages… to show only entries in specific stages — pick several at once; each appears as a colored chip.
  4. Click Clear Filters to reset the view.

images.alt: Dial List Entries toolbar with Show All, Show Pending, Show Completed, Delete Selected, and Refresh buttons
images.caption: The Dial List toolbar — flip between All, Pending, and Completed views

images.alt: Dial List Entries toolbar with Show All, Show Pending, Show Completed, Delete Selected, and Refresh buttons
images.caption: The Dial List toolbar — flip between All, Pending, and Completed views

images.alt: Search box and lifecycle stage filter above the dial list table
images.caption: Search by name or phone, or narrow the list to specific lifecycle stages

images.alt: Search box and lifecycle stage filter above the dial list table
images.caption: Search by name or phone, or narrow the list to specific lifecycle stages

steps.title: Control retries — how many times each number gets called
steps.content:
No-answers happen. The campaign automatically re-queues them so real prospects don't slip away — and you decide exactly how persistent to be, in the Settings tab under Retry Settings:

  • Max Dials Per Phone Number — how many total attempts each number gets (0–5). This is the second half of the Attempts column: with a value of 3, ten people means up to 30 total attempts.
  • Retry Delay (minutes) — how long to wait before calling the same number again.
  • All Numbers for Lead/Contact Stop After — pick stages that stop every number for that person. If the AI marks someone "Qualified" on their mobile, their office line won't be dialed either.
  • Individual Number Stop Retrying — pick stages that stop retries for just that one number while other numbers for the same person keep going.
⚠️ Retries only fire inside the campaign's Working Hours (also on the Settings tab), and numbers on your Do Not Call list are skipped automatically — you never have to prune them by hand.

images.alt: Retry Settings card with Max Dials Per Phone Number, Retry Delay, and stage-based stop rules
images.caption: Retry Settings: attempts per number, delay between tries, and stages that stop retrying

images.alt: Retry Settings card with Max Dials Per Phone Number, Retry Delay, and stage-based stop rules
images.caption: Retry Settings: attempts per number, delay between tries, and stages that stop retrying

steps.title: Track progress on the Overview tab
steps.content:
Once the campaign is running, the Overview tab turns your dial list into a live scoreboard:

  • Contacts — unique people in the queue.
  • Attempts Made — dials placed vs. the total possible (contacts × max dials).
  • Attempts Left — how much runway remains.
  • Finished — entries fully dialed, including how many completed early because they hit a stop stage.
  • Progress — percentage complete, with a progress bar.
  • Live Calls — calls happening right now.

Below the tiles, Next Scheduled Calls lists exactly who is up next and when — click any name to open their record.

images.alt: Current Dial List stats: Contacts, Attempts Made, Attempts Left, Finished, Progress, and Live Calls
images.caption: The Overview scoreboard — 10 contacts × 3 max dials = 30 possible attempts

images.alt: Current Dial List stats: Contacts, Attempts Made, Attempts Left, Finished, Progress, and Live Calls
images.caption: The Overview scoreboard — 10 contacts × 3 max dials = 30 possible attempts

steps.title: Clean up entries — and what happens when it's done
steps.content:
Keep the queue lean while the campaign is live:

  1. On the Dial List tab, tick the entries you no longer want to call.
  2. Click Delete Selected. Selecting every row on a page offers a one-click Select all entries across all pages for bulk cleanup.

When every entry has been fully attempted, the campaign flips to Completed and the dial list becomes a read-only archive — you'll see an Archive view banner, checkboxes are disabled, and no new people can be added. Campaigns left paused or in draft for a long time are auto-archived the same way (you'll get an on-screen warning first, with Resume and Pin buttons to keep them active).

💡 Want to run the same audience again? Open a completed campaign's Add Leads or Add Contacts tab and click Clone Campaign — you get a fresh copy ready for a new run, while the archived original stays untouched as your permanent record. Your list, your rules, zero wasted dials — that's how winning call campaigns are run.
